title: Mister Fantastic
content:
{{SomethingElse|the [[Earth-838]] [[Variant]]|Mister Fantastic/Earth-838|Mister Fantastic}} {{CharHub|Mister Fantastic|Reed Richards}} {{AltUniverse|[[Earth-828]]|3=Characters}} {{Stub}} {{Character |image = Mister Fantastic Infobox.png |real name = Reed Richards{{Ref|FFFS}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Mister Fantastic{{Ref|FFFS}} |nicknames = Stretch{{Ref|FFFS}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |title = Doctor
Professor |affiliation = {{Affiliation|RT}}
{{Affiliation|ANSA}}
{{Affiliation|FF}}
{{Affiliation|FUFO}} |movie = ''[[The Fantastic Four: First Steps]]''
''[[Avengers: Doomsday]]'' (unreleased)[https://x.com/DiscussingFilm/status/1817378767787975016 DiscussingFilm on X - July 27, 2024]
''[[Avengers: Secret Wars]]'' (unreleased) |web series = ''[[Future Foundation Public Archives/Promotional Campaign|Future Foundation Public Archives]]'' |comic = ''[[Fantastic Four: First Steps]]''
''[[Fantastic Four: First Foes]]'' (unreleased)[https://thedirect.com/article/mcu-fantastic-four-movie-first-foes-prequel-comic Marvel Unveils Prequel to MCU Fantastic Four Movie, Announces 'First Foes' Comic (Exclusive)] |actor = [[Pedro Pascal]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|It is my job to think terrible things so that terrible things don't happen."
"That's not your job, Reed. That's you."
"Okay. You're right, it is me. I don't dream. I don't wonder. I invite the worst possible thing into my head to figure out how to hurt them before they hurt anybody else!|Reed Richards and [[Sue Storm]]|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Doctor '''Reed Richards''' is the husband of [[Sue Storm]], a world-famous, extraordinarily intelligent scientist, and the founding leader of the [[Fantastic Four]] named '''Mister Fantastic''', who can stretch his body to various proportions. Four years after his team's formation, Richards and Storm were expecting a son named [[Franklin Richards]], and eventually, they were warned by [[Silver Surfer]] of [[Galactus]]' intent to devour [[Earth]]. The Fantastic Four attempted to reason with Galactus, but refused when he demanded for Mister Fantastic's son in exchange for the planet's safety, causing personal conflicts and complications for him. He soon attempted to use [[Teleportation Bridge]]s around the world to teleport Earth far away from Galactus, though this plan was foiled when Silver Surfer sabotaged nearly all the Bridges. Instead, when Galactus arrived in [[New York City]] to claim Franklin, Mister Fantastic and his team successfully used a Bridge to banish Galactus far into space, saving his family and the world. ==Biography== {{MD|Reed Richards (Earth-828)}} ===Early Life=== ====Teaching Science==== Richards earned PhDs in astrophysics, engineering, and mathematics. He became a teacher and mentor, priding himself in that work as he saw many of his students go on to become successful scientists in their own right.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Construction of the Baxter Building==== {{Quote|We had no idea construction of the [[Baxter Building]] would provoke [[Subterranea]], because we had no idea Subterranea EXISTED. It was as if we'd stepped into a hornet's nest. Only in a place no one knew a hornet's nest could exist.|Reed Richards|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Richards was involved in the construction of the [[Baxter Building]] in [[New York City]], having planned where it would be and designed it. Richards was unaware, however, that there was a [[Subterranea|society]] underneath it that would be disrupted by the construction.{{Ref|FFFS}} ===Founding the Fantastic Four=== ====Gaining Abilities==== [[File:Fantastic Four Astronauts.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Richards, [[Ben Grimm|Grimm]], [[Sue Storm|Sue]], and [[Johnny Storm|Johnny]] prepare for launch]] {{Quote|Four years ago, [[Fantastic Four|we]] were dreamers. Back then, the unknown meant adventure and mystery, and discovery. And space, the greatest mystery of all. What we learned was that the unknown also meant fear. And profound, radical change.|Mister Fantastic|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Richards built a spacecraft far more advanced than anything which existed at the time, working alongside engineers and technicians with a hands-on approach. The construction process led to the invention of a lightweight, heat-resistant alloy for the ship. Richards, along with [[Sue Storm]], [[Johnny Storm]], and [[Ben Grimm]], took it into space, hoping to gather data on [[Cosmic Radiation]]. [[File:Cosmic Radiation.png|{{AltFloat}}|Richards and his cohort get bombarded with [[Cosmic Radiation]]]] However, while they were in space, an unanticipated storm of cosmic radiation bombarded the ship and got through it's defenses. The radiation gave each of them a unique superpower, with Richards' body becoming elastic. The four returned to [[Earth]], and Richards insisted that they isolate themselves so that they can better understand their changed physiognomies and ensure they were not a danger to those around them. They went to [[Huntsville|Rocket City]] and stayed in [[Building Q]].{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Isolation==== {{Quote|[[Fantastic Four|Our]] period of self-quarantine allowed us time to better understand how our physiognomies had changed while ensuring we were not a danger to anyone else.|Reed Richards|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} Having spent a long time in isolation, [[Johnny Storm]] asked when they would be allowed to leave. [[Sue Storm]], referring to Richards as "Dr. Richards," made a comment about Richards not telling them how long it would be, and Richards told Sue not to be so formal with him while saying that he would indicate when it was time to leave quarantine. Meanwhile, [[Ben Grimm]] broke [[K.E.R.B.I.E.]], leading to a fight between him and Johnny. Richards attempted to intervene, getting involved in the fight as he wrapped his body around Johnny and Grimm, asking Sue for help. Sue was distracted, but then noticed what happened and put all three men in force fields, depriving of them of oxygen momentarily and forcing them to stop. [[File:Fantasti-Car (The Fantastic Four First Steps).jpg|{{AltFloat|s=200}}|Richards and his family leave [[Building Q]]]] Once they recovered, Sue told them all that there was a situation in [[New York City]], directing their attention to the computer. They saw news footage of [[Moloids]] [[Revolt of Subterranea|attacking]] the surface world, shocking them all. Grimm told Richards that it would have to be time to leave. Richards felt conflicted about leaving, understanding as a scientist that they were all stable while still feeling concern for the people he had come to consider a family. The team got into the [[Fantasticar]] and left Building Q, heading toward New York.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====[[Revolt of Subterranea]]==== [[File:Fantastic Four vs Giganto (6).png|{{AltFloat}}|Reed and the [[Fantastic Four|team]] defeats [[Giganto]]]] {{Quote|It's gonna freak people out, [[Sue Storm|Sue]]."
"Join the club, Storm.|[[Johnny Storm]] and Reed Richards|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} The four landed in [[New York City]] and were immediately surrounded by paparazzi, which made Richards uncomfortable. However, he put his personal discomfort aside so they could defend the city. They all looked into a large hole in the ground, when suddenly [[Giganto]] appeared out of it and began attacking. [[Ben Grimm]] charged the monster, but Richards shouted at him for stop since they did not have a plan. However, Grimm simply said they needed to clobber Giganto. [[Johnny Storm]] was worried about causing panic with their powers, but Richards said that they were all feeling that way and encouraged Storm to help. Richards wrapped himself around Giganto as they all fought, and [[Sue Storm]] eventually used a forcefield to suffocate Giganto. [[File:Mole Man decides to unleash the Revolt of Subterranea.jpg|{{AltFloat|s=200}}|Richards and the [[Fantastic Four]] confront [[Harvey Elder]]]] Richards regrouped with the rest of the team, when suddenly the [[Pan Am Building]] fell underground, having been stolen by their attackers, making Richards afraid. However, the team reached where the building stood and went underground anyway. As they reached the ground floor of the Pan Am Building, [[Harvey Elder]] ordered the [[Moloids]] to attack. Richards began wrapping himself around Elder and complimented the technology needed to steal the Pan Am Building, but Elder cut Richards off and called him an oppressor. Richards shouted a warning to Sue Storm as a pregnant Moloid attacked her with a pick. Storm trapped the Moloid in a force field, and Elder pleaded with Richards to let the pregnant woman go in exchange for Elder's surrender, so Richards demanded that Elder prove his sincerity. Elder called off the attack and declared himself a political prisoner as Richards bound Elder's arms together with his body. Elder was moved into one of Storm's forcefields as the team walked away, and Grimm noted that the Moloid that attacked Storm was pregnant. Richards said that it was absurd to fight in such a condition, leading Grimm to note that Richards had a rigid way of thinking.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Establishing Peace==== [[Sue Storm]] talked to [[Harvey Elder]] while Elder was in custody and agreed to help Elder reestablish his society underground in a way that kept both Elder's people and [[humans]] happy. While Storm worked to establish sovereignty for [[Subterranea]] with the [[United Nations/Earth-828|United Nations]], Richards spoke with the [[United States Congress/Earth-828|United States Congress]] in working to establish peace, saying that he did not know the construction of the [[Baxter Building]] would affect Subterranea since he did not know Subterranea existed. He likened the situation to stepping in a hornet's nest when in a place people did not know hornet's nests could be. He also talked to the [[New York City Council/Earth-828|New York City Council]], explaining that [[Giganto]]'s attack was only a small portion of the fight and that he felt real fear when realizing the full scale of the [[Revolt of Subterranea]]. Despite this, he continued to work to build the peaceful nature between the two peoples and helped to rebuild Subterranea. The [[Fantastic Four]] moved into the [[Baxter Building]] full time and hosted the Moloids, which Richards enjoyed. The team held a ribbon cutting ceremony and celebrated their new building.{{Ref|FFFS}} ===Fame=== ====Interviews==== Richards was interviewed about his period of isolation and the [[Revolt of Subterranea]] many times, including by ''[[Science Dad]]'' for their magazine. Richards told ''Science Dad'' about how their period of isolation allowed them to understand their changes and ensure they were not dangerous. In an interview for ''[[Timely]]'', Richards talked about his conflicted feelings regarding leaving [[Building Q]] for the first time, referring to the [[Fantastic Four]] as his "cohort." [[Sue Storm]] later said in a telecast that Richards intended to say "family." While talking to ''[[The Introspective Introvert Hour]]'', Richards expressed concern with [[Harvey Elder]], who he mistakenly called "Mole Man," declaring war on the surface world and said that scientists could not be involved in war. While on ''[[The Ted Gilbert Report]]'', Richards said that the team's own personal discomfort during the revolt did not matter due to the need to protect the city.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Advancing Scientific Study==== Richards continued his work in science, expanding his expertise to include chemistry and biology. He became and advisor to the [[American National Space Agency]], helping the [[United States]]' space program grow. He developed theoretical models on propulsion systems with the hopes of being able to land on the moon. He also became notable as a biologist, making discoveries about DNA and gene manipulation that promised to eradicate genetic diseases and improve crop yields. He also worked to advocate for more ethical scientific practices, encouraging scientists to focus on the societal implications of their work. He also advocated for international cooperation on space exploration, hoping to establish permanent space stations and colonies on the [[moon]]. At one point, Richards delivered a lecture titled ''[[Unstable Molecules: Modern Superscience and Modern Superscientist]]'' at the [[314 St. YMCA]], talking about [[Unstable Molecules]]. Talking about the [[Revolt of Subterranea]], he explained how much bigger the world suddenly got, saying that while most scientists would dream of that, it had become Richards' nightmare.{{Ref|FFFS}} ====Expecting a New Member==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ===[[Advent of Galactus]]=== ====Encountering Silver Surfer==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ====Preparing for Launch==== {{Quote|The four of us will return to space and face the danger waiting there, and we will find this '[[Galactus]].' Here we stand, prepared to face the danger that is unknown. The danger that's threatened our home, threatened our families, and threatened this [[Earth|planet]]. The unknown will become known, and we will protect you. We will protect you.|Mister Fantastic|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ====[[Destruction of LHS-275|Visiting LHS-275]]==== {{Quote|[[Galactus]] will see you."
"I'll go."
"All of you... You should not have come.|[[Silver Surfer]] and Reed Richards|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ====[[Chase of the Fantastic Four|Escape from Silver Surfer]]==== {{Quote|You will not have our [[Earth|planet]] and you will never have our [[Franklin Richards|son]]!"
"I will eat your planet slowly while your child watches.|Reed Richards and [[Galactus]]|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ====Breakers of Bad News==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ====Encouraging the World==== {{Quote|With [[Galactus]]' [[Taa II|ship]] deep within our [[Solar System]], and the Fantastic Four's 'Hail Mary' in the balance, we are calling on every helping hand. If you can do something well, now you can do it for your [[Earth|planet]].|Reed Richards|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ====[[Battle of New York/Earth-828|Battle of New York]]==== {{Quote|Sue, stop!"
"Save our [[Franklin Richards|son]]!|Mister Fantastic and [[Invisible Woman]]|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ===Continuing to Change=== ====New Adventure==== ''To be added''{{Ref|TFFFS}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== *'''Artificially Enhanced Physiology''': While on a mission to space, Reed Richards was subjected to [[Cosmic Radiation]], which altered his DNA and gave him the ability to control the elasticity and flexibility of his entire body. **'''Elasticity''': Richards has the ability to stretch, bend, twist, flatten, deform, inflate, and contract his entire body. His body is also very resilient and durable to injury. ***'''Superhuman Flexibility''': Richards was seen holding Gigantos' giant hand back before Ben punched him in the throat and knocked him out. ***'''Superhuman Agility''': Richards was seen swiftly escaping a fiery explosion inside a building. He was later seen quickly scaling up a wifi tower to defeat one of Red Ghost's Super-Apes. ***'''Superhuman Leaping''': Richards ability to stretch his arms and legs allows him to propel himself very high. ***'''Superhuman Durability''': Richards was able to survive being stretched by Galactus to the point of snapping. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|This equation not only confirms alternate dimensions, it suggests that parallel Earths exist on different dimensional planes...|Reed Richards|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': Reed Richards is able to decipher the existence in the [[Multiverse]] of both dimensions and parallel universes through simple equations. {{Quote|What sets Reed Richards apart is not just his scientific acumen but also his vision for the future. He is a firm believer in the power of science to improve humanity and is dedicated to ensuring that technological advancements are used for the greater good.|''[[School of Science Newsletter]]''|Fantastic Four: First Steps}} *'''Master Scientist''': Reed Richards is one of the most brilliant scientists of all time, having acknowledged the very existence of the [[Multiverse]] and even created [[Teleportation Bridge]] despite the scientific and technological means of his time. *'''Master Engineer''': Reed Richards being one of the most brilliant minds of all time, had built many impressive vehicles and inventions, often building specialized suits for his team, vehicles such as the Excelsior and Fantasticar, and even a special scanner to detect anomalies in their child. *'''Expert Tactician''': Reed Richards, as the leader of the Fantastic Four, is a highly calculative and intelligent, being able to guide the team to prepare for Galactus, save their child, and came up with a way save the Earth from Galactus stealing [[Franklin Richards|Franklin]] and destroying the world. *'''Expert Businessman''': ''To be added'' *'''Expert Acrobat''': ''To be added'' ==Equipment== *'''[[Fantastic Four Uniforms|Fantastic Four Uniform]]''': Reed wears a suit that is able to stretch with him but it has limitations as it can still be torn. *'''[[Digi-Calc]]''': ''To be added'' *'''[[Interna-Scan]]''': ''To be added'' *'''[[Teleportation Bridge]]''': ''To be added'' ===Vehicles=== *'''[[Excelsior]]''': Reed serves as the main pilot of the ship. *'''[[Fantasticar]]''': ''To be added'' ==Facilities== {{Quote|[[Fantastic Four|We]] make a point to do family dinner every week. Sunday at seven on the dot. No matter what, we're all here.|Reed Richards|The Fantastic Four: First Steps}} *'''[[Baxter Building]]''': ''To be added'' ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[John F. Storm]] † - Grandfather-in-Law *[[Franklin Storm]] - Father-in-Law *[[Mary Storm]] † - Mother-in-Law *[[Invisible Woman|Sue Storm/Invisible Woman]] - Wife and Teammate *[[Human Torch|Johnny Storm/Human Torch]] - Brother-in-Law and Teammate *[[Franklin Richards]] - Son ===Allies=== *[[Fantastic Four]] **[[Thing|Ben Grimm/Thing]] - Teammate and Best Friend *[[K.E.R.B.I.E.]] † - Creation *[[H.E.R.B.I.E.]] - Creation and Assistant *[[John F. Kennedy/Earth-828|John F. Kennedy]] *[[Jackie Kennedy]] *[[New York City Council/Earth-828|New York City Council]] *[[ReedTech]] *[[American National Space Agency]] *[[Future Foundation]] **[[Lynne Nichols]] *[[American Broadcasting Company/Earth-828|American Broadcasting Company]] **[[William Russell]] *''[[Post]]'' **[[Austin Thorogold]] *[[Mole Man|Harvey Elder/Mole Man]] - Former Enemy *[[Moloids]] - Former Enemies *''[[Science Dad]]'' - Interviewers *''[[Timely]]'' - Interviewers *''[[The Introspective Introvert Hour]]'' - Interviewers *''[[The Ted Gilbert Report]]'' *''[[The Ted Gilbert Show]]'' **[[Ted Gilbert]] - Interviewer **[[Sally (Makeup Artist)|Sally]] *[[United States Congress/Earth-828|United States Congress]] *[[New York City Police Department/Earth-828|New York City Police Department]] *[[Fire Department of New York City/Earth-828|Fire Department of New York City]] *[[Donna (Child)|Donna]] *[[Silver Surfer|Shalla-Bal/Silver Surfer]] - Former Enemy {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Giganto]] *[[Mad Thinker|Julius/Mad Thinker]] *[[Diablo|Esteban Corazón de Pablo/Diablo]] *[[Puppet Master|Phillip Masters/Puppet Master]] *[[Wizard|Bentley Wittman/Wizard]] *[[Red Ghost|Ivan Kragoff/Red Ghost]] *[[Super-Apes]] **[[Peotor]] **[[Igor]] *[[Galactus|Galan/Galactus]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|TFFFS}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|FFFS}} **{{Appearance|TFFFS}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|FFFF}} (unreleased) *{{Appearance|TFFFS}} **{{Appearance|FFPA}} (concurrent events) *{{Appearance|AD}} (unreleased) *{{Appearance|ASW}} (unreleased) ==Trivia== *In the comics, Doctor Reed Richards is the co-founder and leader of the [[Fantastic Four]], having gained his superhuman elasticity abilities from enduring [[Cosmic Radiation|cosmic irradiation]] during a space voyage on a [[Excelsior|shuttle]] he commandeered with his other three colleagues. He is revered as the smartest man in the world and has a long rivalry with [[Doctor Doom]]. ==Behind the Scenes== *Mister Fantastic in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] is the fifth live-action incarnation of the character, following incarnations portrayed by [[Alex Hyde-White]], {{WPS|Ioan Gruffudd}}, {{WPS|Miles Teller}} and [[John Krasinski]]. *{{WPS|Rahul Kohli}} was a runner-up for the role of Mister Fantastic.{{cite}} *''[[The Fantastic Four: First Steps]]'' director [[Matt Shakman]] has described Mister Fantastic as the "''the most scientifically intelligent person''" of his [[Earth]] and a combination of {{WPS|Steve Jobs}}, {{WPS|Albert Einstein}} and {{WPS|Robert Moses}}.[https://ew.com/the-fantastic-four-first-steps-mcu-plot-character-details-cover-story-11712063 Welcome to the (retro)future: Inside the Fantastic Four's 'first steps' into the MCU] ==References== {{Gallery|Mister Fantastic/Gallery}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} [[es:Reed Richards]] [[pt-br:Senhor Fantástico]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:The Fantastic Four: First Steps Characters]] [[Category:Avengers: Doomsday Characters]] [[Category:Avengers: Secret Wars Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Black Hair]] [[Category:Gray Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:Executives]] [[Category:Astronauts]] [[Category:Professors]] [[Category:ReedTech Leaders]] [[Category:American National Space Agency Members]] [[Category:Fantastic Four Leaders]] [[Category:Future Foundation Members]] [[Category:Enhanced Individuals]]
